privileged_info_summary: This task operates on real, non-synthetic legal data: 7 landmark US Supreme Court cases (Brown v. Board, Marbury v. Madison, Miranda v. Arizona, New York Times Co. v. Sullivan, Gideon v. Wainwright, Obergefell v. Hodges, Tinker v. Des Moines) and 3 published law review articles (The Path of the Law by Holmes, Constitutional Fact Review by Monaghan, and The Due Process Clause and the Substantive Law of Torts by Poe). All 7 Supreme Court cases and 2 of the 3 law review articles are independently verified as real, published, historical legal sources. The collection names (Constitutional Law Seminar, First Amendment, Due Process, Judicial Review) are task-defined organizational categories, not real collections. The task involves organizing these 10 real legal references into a hierarchical collection structure within Jurism (a legal research database).
